title: Evangelion dialogue line statistical report
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion: dialogue line statistical report             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Thu Oct 23, 2008 2:08 pm Found this today while browsing for Eva news: http://memo256.picotips.com/evangelion_line_analysis/ How many words each character speaks, and the percentage of each character's interaction with each other. The biggest loudmouth seems to be Misato, or probably Asuka which ranks 4º while entering the series at #8. Discuss...

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Oct 23, 2008 2:31 pm That's pretty interesting.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Oct 23, 2008 3:07 pm Very interesting! And Shinji has the most interactions with Asuka huh? I actually thought it was evenly divided with how much he spoke to the main cast. Thanks for this, it's a nice skim thru.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Oct 23, 2008 3:39 pm try running it through Google translator ... heh! I'm not getting the second chart at all. Seriously, when the hell did Gendo talk to Kensuke?

[]:             Quote Post by mrpanda » Thu Oct 23, 2008 3:39 pm Any possibility of a translation into english as this looks quite interesting ?

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Fri Oct 24, 2008 12:01 pm The first chart is ammount of words spoke by character by each episode. Second chart shows totals of the first chart, ordered from max to min. Third chart shows percentage of interaction between characters. Of the character's total interaction (all instances of the chara changing lines with another one, xcept I think minor or secondary charas), percentage per chara interacted to. The only translation necessary beyond that, I believe, are the charas names. Lemme try: ミサト - Misato シンジ - Shinji リツコ - Ritsuko アスカ - Asuka マヤ - Maya ゲンドウ - Gendou 加持 - Kaji 冬月 - Fuyutsuki レイ - Rei マコト - Makoto ケンスケ - Kensuke トウジ - Touji シゲル - Shigeru ヒカリ - Hikari カヲル - Kaworu キール - Keel ユイ - Yui

[]:             Quote Post by V » Fri Oct 24, 2008 12:22 pm when they say "23183" for Misato, I assume that is "number of speaking lines?" or is it "number of words"? it seems too high to be speaking linese Maya talks more than Gendo did? well, not so surprising given that she gets so much "background filler" technical dialogue why do they apparently use Gendo-Fuyutsuki dialogue as their standard for 100%?

[]:             Quote Post by Zaiku » Fri Oct 24, 2008 10:12 pm NemZ wrote: try running it through Google translator ... heh! I'm not getting the second chart at all. Seriously, when the hell did Gendo talk to Kensuke?

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sat Oct 25, 2008 3:19 am Don't expect a robo translator to be able to get names correctly. Japanese names (and Chinese names for the more part) involve different readings of kanji or a mix of kanji, hiragani, or katakana. Sometimes, even 2 people with different names could have the same kanji in their written name. Politicians in Japan almost always have a very simple name, otherwise, people who want to vote for them would never be able to write it (voting there is by ballot and you write the candidate's name you want to vote for). This is why candidates walk around with big banners of their names. Better explanation here . I remember when Asuka's name would get translated by babelfish as "ground mosquito".

[]:             Quote Post by pat457 » Sat Oct 25, 2008 3:49 am Zaiku wrote: Mi Sato Co., Ltd.? Ray? Winter Months? xD

[]:             Quote Post by Synapsid » Sat Oct 25, 2008 11:51 am Zaiku wrote: NemZ wrote: try running it through Google translator ... heh! I'm not getting the second chart at all. Seriously, when the hell did Gendo talk to Kensuke?

[]:             Quote Post by Zaiku » Sat Oct 25, 2008 3:20 pm Wow, for a main character, Rei doesn't say much.

[]:             Quote Post by pat457 » Sat Oct 25, 2008 7:54 pm Zaiku wrote: Wow, for a main character, Rei doesn't say much.

[]:             Quote Post by Zaiku » Mon Oct 27, 2008 1:26 am ^Yeah, but still... She has less lines than Maya and Kensuke... MAYA AND FREAKIN' KENSUKE!

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Tue Oct 28, 2008 7:29 am The first two charts are about "words", I believe. 'Tsays "文字数推移" Mojisuu suii, something about number of characters. They can differ whether they took more or less kanji, or more or less kanas.
